name.description: The name of the DS8000 host to work with.

state.description: Specify the state the DS8000 host should be in.

host_type.description: The host type of the host that will be created on the DS8000 storage system.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_host_info.description: Return information pertaining to DS8000 hosts. If the optional parameters are not set, information on all hosts on the DS8000 storage system will be returned.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_host_port.short_description: Manage host ports for a DS8000 host
ibm.ds8000.ds8000_host_port.description: Manage host ports for a DS8000 host.

name.description: The name of the DS8000 host to work with. Required when I(state=present).

state.description: Specify the state the DS8000 host port should be in.

host_port.description: List of host port WWPNs to assign to a specific host on the DS8000 storage system.

force.description: Optional when I(state=present). If C(yes), if a host port is assigned to another host, it will transfer it to the host specified. If C(no), if a host port is assigned to another host, it will not transfer it to the host specified.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_host_port_info.short_description: Return info on DS8000 host ports
ibm.ds8000.ds8000_host_port_info.description: Return information pertaining to DS8000 host ports. If the optional parameters are not set, information on all host ports on the DS8000 storage system will be returned.

host_port.description: List of host port WWPNs to query.

host.description: The name of the DS8000 host to query.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_lss.description: Manage a DS8000 storage image logical subsystem (lss), also known as a logical control unit (lcu). A lcu is configured to represent a grouping of logical CKD volumes. The DS8000 has a 64 KB 256 volume address space that is partitioned into 255 lss units, where each lss contains 256 logical volume numbers. The 255 lss units are assigned to one of 16 address groups, where each address group contains 16 lsses, or 4 KB volume addresses. lcus are typically created in groups of 16, beginning at lss address 00.

id.description: The lss ID to be created. An lss ID is two hexadecimal characters 00 - FE.

state.description: Specify the state the DS8000 lss should be in.

ssid.description: The subsystem ID that you assign. A subsystem ID is four hexadecimal characters 0000-FFFF. Required when I(state=present)

ckd_type.description: The type of lss to create.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_lss_info.description: Return information pertaining to DS8000 lsses. If the optional parameters are not set, information on all lsses on the DS8000 storage system will be returned.

id.description: The logical subsystem (lss) ID to be queried. An lss ID is two hexadecimal characters 00 - FE.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_marray_info.short_description: Return info on DS8000 managed arrays
ibm.ds8000.ds8000_marray_info.description: Return information pertaining to DS8000 managed arrays. If the optional parameters are not set, information on all managed arrays on the DS8000 storage system will be returned.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_pool_info.description: Return information pertaining to DS8000 pools. If the optional parameters are not set, information on all pools on the DS8000 storage system will be returned.

label.description: The resource group label is 1 to 32 characters and is limited to upper and lower case alphabetic and numeric characters, and the special characters (-), (_), and (.). Required when I(state=present)

state.description: Specify the state the DS8000 resource group should be in.

name.description: The nickname that you assigned for this resource group object.

id.description: Unique identifier that is assigned to this resource group ID in the form RG If not specified, one will be chosen. Resource Group 0 is predefined and cannot be created, deleted, or modified. By default, all resources belong to this group unless otherwise specified.

cs_global.description: All of the Copy Services requests that establish or re-establish a relationship, and are subject to this resource scope including FlashCopy and PPRC relationships.

pass_global.description: All of the Copy Services requests that are issued though a pass-through logical volume are treated as a relationship between the pass-through logical volume and source logical volume and are subject to this resource scope.

gm_masters.description: An array of Global Mirror session IDs that are allowed to be used as a master session for volumes in this resource. A Session ID is a hexadecimal number in the 01 - FF range.

gm_sessions.description: An array of Global Mirror session IDs that are allowed to be used for the volumes in this resource. A Session ID is a hexadecimal number in the 01 - FF range.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_resource_group_info.short_description: Return info on DS8000 resource groups
ibm.ds8000.ds8000_resource_group_info.description: Return info on DS8000 resource groups.

id.description: The unique identifier that is assigned to this resource group ID in the form RG\#.

name.description: The name of the DS8000 volume to work with. Required when I(state=present)

state.description: Specify the state the DS8000 volume should be in.

id.description: The volume IDs of the DS8000 volume to work with. Required when I(state=absent) Only one element is allowed when I(alias=yes)

volume_type.description: The volume type that will be created. Valid value is fixed block C(fb) or count key data C(ckd).

capacity.description: The size of the volume. Required when I(state=present)

capacity_type.description: The units of measurement of the size of the volume.

lss.description: The logical subsystem (lss) that the volume will be created on.

pool.description: The pool id that the volume will be created on. Required when I(state=present)

storage_allocation_method.description: Choose the storage allocation method that the DS8000 will use in creating your volume. Valid value is C(none), extent space-efficient C(ese), track space-efficient C(tse).

quantity.description: The number of volumes that will be created.

alias.description: Boolean specifying if the id is an alias

alias_order.description: The order in which alias volume IDs are assigned.

ckd_base_ids.description: List of existing base CKD volume IDs to create aliases for.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_volume_info.description: Return information pertaining to DS8000 volumes. If the optional parameters are not set, information on all volumes on the DS8000 storage system will be returned.

host.description: The host that the volumes are mapped to.

pool.description: The pool id that the volumes belong to.

lss.description: The lss id that the volumes belong to.

ibm.ds8000.ds8000_volume_mapping.short_description: Manage DS8000 volume mapping to hosts
ibm.ds8000.ds8000_volume_mapping.description: Manage DS8000 volume mapping to hosts.

name.description: The name of the DS8000 host to work with.

state.description: Specify the state the DS8000 volume mapping should be in.

volume_id.description: The volume ID of the volume that you want to map to a host.

volume_name.description: The volume name that you want to map to a host. Notice that different volumes sometimes have the same volume name, so it will map all of them. To use a specific volume, use I(volume_id)
